title: angelica pseudoselinum
intro: Angelica pseudoselinum, also known as Wild Celery, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its tall stalks and umbrella-like clusters of small white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic qualities, which make it effective in treating a variety of health issues. The health benefits of Wild Celery include reducing fever, alleviating digestive problems, and relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Wild Celery is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in cooking, adding its subtle flavor to soups, stews, and salads.
